channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Injured but Secure Amid Conflict
item.description: Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Ayatullah Mojtaba Khamenei, has reportedly been injured but remains safe, according to the son of President Masoud Pezeshkian. Mojtaba, 56, rose to the top post ...

item.title: At least six dead after bus fire in Kerzers, Switzerland; authorities investigate deliberate act
item.description: At least six people have died and three others were injured after a bus caught fire in Kerzers, in the canton of Fribourg, western Switzerland. Police suspect the blaze may have been a deliberate act,...

item.title: Labour MP speaks out on delays in rape cases and justice reforms
item.description: Labour MP Charlotte Nichols revealed in the House of Commons that it took 1,088 days for her rape case to reach court, highlighting the prolonged delays faced by victims. She disclosed that she was ra...

item.title: Sifuna Claims Raila Tasked Him to Oversee ODM-UDA Political Pact, Disputes Ruto's Remarks
item.description: Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna has come out clear on his role in the ODM-UDA political arrangement. Speaking during the launch of a 'People's Report' in Nairobi, Sifuna said he was personally appointed ...

item.title: How Duterte’s online support strategies continue a year after his ICC arrest
item.description: A year after former President Rodrigo Duterte’s arrest by the International Criminal Court (ICC) over alleged crimes against humanity, patterns of online support and narrative-shaping continue to pers...

item.title: EFCC Interrogates Chief Judge Tsoho Over $160,000 Found With Wife
item.description: On Tuesday, the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) conducted a thorough questioning of the Chief Judge of the Federal High Court, Justice John Tsoho, over a large sum of money found with ...

item.title: PM Modi assures full support for Indians in Gulf amid Middle East tensions and criticises Congress
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while addressing a public rally in Kochi, Kerala, assured that the Indian government is providing full support to citizens stranded in the Gulf region amid ongoing tensio...

item.title: Boncana Maïga and the Evolution of African Salsa
item.description: Boncana Maïga, the celebrated Malian flautist, composer and arranger, passed away at 77, leaving a lasting legacy in West African music. Born in Gao, Mali, Maïga taught himself to play the saxophone a...

item.title: Coordinated Response Needed to Address Rising Antisemitic Attacks in Canada
item.description: Following a series of violent attacks on Toronto-area synagogues, including shootings, firebombings, and vandalism, Canadian authorities have pledged to take action against rising antisemitism. Prime ...

item.title: Supreme Court Allows Passive Euthanasia for Harish Rana After 13 Years in Vegetative State
item.description: In a historic judgment delivered on 11 March 2026, the Supreme Court of India allowed the withdrawal of life support for Harish Rana, a 32-year-old man who had been in a permanent vegetative state for...

item.title: Chennai residents anxious over LPG supply amid West Asia conflict
item.description: Residents of Chennai are facing growing concern over the supply of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) as tensions escalate in West Asia, which has disrupted imports. Despite the Union government taking pro...

item.title: Uttar Pradesh woman allegedly poisons daughter and implicates boyfriend
item.description: In a shocking incident from Uttar Pradesh, a woman allegedly attempted to poison her own daughter because she disapproved of her daughter's relationship. Authorities reported that after the poisoning ...

item.title: Kyle and Jackie O resume contact amid ongoing contract dispute at KIIS FM
item.description: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson have reportedly resumed contact nearly three weeks after a highly publicised on-air argument that put their long-running show at KIIS FM in jeopardy. Sandiland...

item.title: Oil prices fluctuate amid uncertainty over US and Israel actions in Iran
item.description: Oil prices are experiencing major ups and downs as traders dey try make sense of mixed information regarding the US and Israel military actions on Iran. Brent crude, the international standard, droppe...

item.title: Lok Sabha Continues Debate on Opposition Resolution Seeking Removal of Speaker Om Birla Amid Heated Exchanges
item.description: On March 11, 2026, the Lok Sabha resumed its proceedings during the ongoing Budget session, with intense discussions continuing on a resolution moved by the Opposition to remove Speaker Om Birla. Oppo...

item.title: Hawston Primary Learner Wins 5th Annual Story Stars Creative Writing Competition
item.description: Zaywin Gillion, a Grade 4 learner at Hawston Primary School, has emerged as the overall winner of the 5th annual Story Stars creative writing competition, hosted by the Western Cape Education Departme...

item.title: Senate Ways and Means Committee Approves in Principle Granting President Marcos Emergency Powers to Suspend or Reduce Fuel Excise Taxes
item.description: The Senate Committee on Ways and Means, chaired by Senator Pia Cayetano, has approved in principle the proposals to grant President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. emergency powers to temporarily susp...

item.title: UK Theatre Sees Record Attendance but Financial Challenges Persist
item.description: UK theatre is experiencing unprecedented popularity, with audiences reaching record levels in 2025. More than 37 million people attended theatre performances across the country, and the West End alone...

item.title: Pakistan Reaffirms Support to Saudi Arabia Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Pakistan has publicly confirmed its readiness to support Saudi Arabia at any time, amid the escalating tensions in the Middle East following the US-Israel conflict with Iran. Prime Minister's spokespe...

item.title: House of Lords to end hereditary peerages following passage of bill
item.description: The House of Lords (Hereditary Peers) Bill has been passed, marking the end of centuries-old parliamentary privileges for hereditary peers. The legislation removes the last 92 hereditary peers, who ha...
